steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 2 argues that Luttrell's conflict with Sempringham Priory was rooted in a property dispute and that he believed he had a rightful claim to the property. The question asks what Text 2's historian would say is necessary for a fair assessment of Luttrell.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — By suggesting that whatever infractions Luttrell might have committed are outweighed by the cultural value of the Luttrell Psalter.: This sidesteps Text 2's core point about Luttrell's belief in his own right. ✗
  • Choice B — By agreeing that though the law might have permitted Luttrell to do so, Luttrell was wrong to have raided Sempringham Priory.: Text 2 does not argue that outside factors unrelated to property rights shaped Luttrell's conduct. ✗
  • Choice C — By pointing out that if Luttrell had taken the actions of which he is accused, then there would be documentation of those actions in the Luttrell Psalter.: Text 2 does not endorse Luttrell's actions unconditionally — it argues his belief in justification must be considered. ✗
  • Choice D — By asserting that a consideration of Luttrell's behavior should take into account that he believed his actions were justified.: This matches Text 2's central claim that Luttrell acted on what he believed was his rightful claim. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
